homeIntro: Southeast Asia's first FRC team — designing, building, and programming competition robots since 2013, and teaching the next generation how to do the same.
aboutStory:
One Degree North began in 2013 as a small group of students tinkering with Lego Mindstorms. Today we are a team of around 30 students who design, machine, wire, and program a full-scale competition robot every season as Southeast Asia's first FRC team.

Our team is built on communication, collaboration, innovation, and sportsmanship. Experienced members mentor new ones, and our alumni — now at schools like UC Berkeley, Duke, Princeton, Brown, and Waterloo — stay involved long after they graduate.

Everyone on the team belongs to a subteam, and every subteam runs its own training modules so knowledge gets passed down season after season.

values.name: Communication
values.detail: Design reviews, match strategy, sponsor pitches — we practice saying it clearly.

values.name: Collaboration
values.detail: Five subteams ship one robot. Nothing works unless it works together.

values.name: Innovation
values.detail: Every season is a new game with no known answers. We prototype early and often.

values.name: Sportsmanship
values.detail: Gracious professionalism on and off the field, win or lose.

milestones.event: Founded at Singapore American School — Southeast Asia's first FRC team.
Regional Engineering Inspiration Award and our first trip to the FIRST Championship in Detroit.
Woodie Flowers Finalist Award at the Southern Cross Regional.
Regional Winners at the Hawaii Regional.
